title: Groove Pool
summary: Apply timing feel (swing, shuffle, groove) to MIDI clips. Drag grooves from the browser onto clips, or extract groove from a reference audio file. The global Groove Amount knob scales all grooves at once.
steps: Open the Groove Pool: click the small 'Hot-Swap Groove' button (wave icon) in the lower-left of the screen, or find grooves in Browser > Grooves
From the browser, drag a groove preset onto a MIDI clip in the Session or Arrangement view
The clip now shows a groove indicator. Adjust Groove Amount (a knob that appears) to scale the effect: 50% = half the groove timing. 100% = full groove timing
Global groove: in the lower-left of the Ableton interface, the global Groove Amount slider scales all grooves in the project simultaneously
Extracting groove from audio: drag an audio file into the clip view, right-click > Extract Groove. The timing imperfections of the audio become a groove preset you can apply to MIDI clips
Commit a groove: with a grooved clip selected, click 'Flatten' — the groove is permanently baked into the MIDI note timing
tips: Straight quantised drums feel robotic. A groove with 20–30% amount adds subtle timing imperfections that make the beat feel more human
The MPC swing presets (MPC 60 Swing 56%) are classic — this is the shuffle feel on most UK garage and house tracks
Extract groove from a vinyl sample: drag a drum loop onto an audio track, extract its groove, apply to your Drum Rack MIDI clip. You capture the swing of the original recording
Apply different groove amounts to different tracks: heavy groove on drums, lighter on bass, no groove on sub. The contrast creates rhythmic interest
practice_hint: In rep-01 or any drum rep, drag an MPC swing groove preset onto the drum MIDI clip. Set Groove Amount to 30%. Compare with 0% (straight quantised) — does the beat feel more natural?
ableton_version_note: Groove Pool available in all editions of Live 9+.
